generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Digital temperature sensor (DS18B20 or similar) measuring internal temperature of the styrofoam box with ±0.5°C precision, interfaced via Raspberry Pi GPIO/I2C protocols"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary digital temperature sensor (DS18B20 or similar) for redundant internal temperature measurement within the styrofoam box, ensuring temperature homogeneity validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Digital temperature sensor measuring ambient conditions outside the styrofoam box to account for environmental influences on thermal dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based thermal model using ordinary differential equations (ODEs) with two free parameters for lumped capacitance approximation of styrofoam box thermal dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelSolverEnabler
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enhanced physics-based thermal model with four free parameters providing improved thermal inertia accuracy for styrofoam box temperature prediction"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelSolverEnabler
]

instance RealTimeTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous time-series temperature data collected at high temporal resolution (1-second intervals) from internal and external sensors for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted1, InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted2, ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingPipelineEnabler, CalibrationAlgorithmEnabler
]

instance HistoricalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated temperature data stored in time-series database for long-term trend analysis and model validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted1, InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted2, ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelRefinementService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded controller acting as cyber-physical interface for sensor/actuator communication via GPIO/I2C protocols, executing control logic in Python"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, DynamicSimulationService, SafetyMonitoringService
]

instance ThermalModelSolverEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Differential equation solver (SciPy/Simulink) executing physics-based thermal models for real-time simulation and prediction"
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicSimulationService, CalibrationService
]

instance DataProcessingPipelineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Streaming analytics pipeline (Apache Kafka + Flink) processing real-time sensor data for feature extraction (temperature gradients, anomalies)"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithmEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Least-squares optimization or Bayesian inference algorithms refining model parameters using real-time sensor data to reduce model-plant mismatch"
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationService
]

instance ModelPredictiveControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-predictive control algorithm dynamically adjusting control parameters for optimal setpoint tracking and robustness"
    DTDFVocab:enables SafetyMonitoringService
]

instance InfluxDBSensorDataStorage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing sensor data with 1-second resolution for historical analysis and model validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelRefinementService
]

instance GrafanaDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring dashboard visualizing temperature profiles, model predictions, and alerts for engineers and operators"
]

instance PythonControlLogic : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based implementation of control logic using numpy/pandas for data processing and differential equation solving"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, DynamicSimulationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous collection and processing of time-series sensor data enabling state estimation and anomaly detection for internal/external temperatures"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureStateInsight, SensorFaultInsight
]

instance DynamicSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of physics-based thermal models to reproduce and predict physical twin behavior under current/perturbed conditions"
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalPredictionInsight, WhatIfAnalysisAction
]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated model parameter refinement using calibration algorithms to ensure predictive accuracy and reduce model-plant mismatch"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, ParameterUpdateAction
]

instance SafetyMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Verification of physical twin operation within safe thermal bounds using supervisory controllers and fault detection algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalSafetyInsight, CorrectiveActionAction
]

instance ReconfigurabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adaptation to changing conditions through runtime adjustment of control strategies using optimization algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemRobustnessInsight, ControlParameterUpdateAction
]

instance AdvancedVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboards providing time-series plots and 3D thermal maps for system state visualization and decision support"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualTemperatureProfileInsight
]

instance ModelRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Iterative model improvement using historical data and calibration results to maintain predictive fidelity over time"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelImprovementInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time estimation of internal/external temperature states with confidence intervals derived from sensor fusion"
]

]

instance ThermalPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted temperature trajectories under current and perturbed operating conditions using physics-based models"
]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of model-plant mismatch through calibration metrics and validation experiments"
]

instance ParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of model parameters (e.g., thermal inertia) based on calibration algorithm outputs"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

]

instance CorrectiveActionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [

]

]

instance ControlParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [

]

instance VisualTemperatureProfileInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization of real-time and predicted temperature profiles with spatial/temporal annotations"
]

instance ModelImprovementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative comparison of model versions (2-parameter vs 4-parameter) and validation against experimental data"
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Resistive heating element generating thermal energy to regulate internal temperature of the styrofoam box"
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical fan distributing heat uniformly within the container to ensure temperature homogeneity"
]

// C1: Physical Components
instance StyrofoamBox : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Insulated container acting as passive thermal barrier to minimize heat loss with two internal temperature sensors"
]

instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Thermal control environment containing all physical components of the CPS-based incubator system"
    base:contains StyrofoamBox, HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT exerts control over the Physical Twin (PT) via the **Raspberry Pi**, which acts as the cyber-physical interface. The **control commands** are generated by the DT’s algorithms (e.g., supervisory controllers, optimizers) and transmitted to the **heatbed** and **fan** to regulate temperature. The **command types** include **temperature setpoints** and **actuation signals** (e.g., power modulation for the heatbed). The documents do not specify **control message formats**, but they imply a structured communication protocol (e.g., serial/TCP/IP) between the cyber and physical layers. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not explicitly described, though the DT’s **calibration algorithms** and **monitors** (Document 1, Fig. 1) suggest implicit validation of commands to ensure safety and optimality. **Feedback systems** are present, as the DT continuously monitors temperature sensors to adjust control actions dynamically. **Conflict resolution** is not detailed, but the DT’s **supervisory controllers** likely prioritize safety-critical commands (e.g., emergency shutdowns) over routine adjustments. **Command execution confirmation** is implied via closed-loop monitoring, where the DT verifies the PT’s response against predicted behavior."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator follows a **Cyber-Physical System (CPS)-based architecture**, integrating **three core components** as outlined in Fig. 1: **data** (collected via **two internal temperature sensors** and **one external sensor**), **models** (thermal simulation models with **2 or 4 free parameters**, calibrated via **calibration algorithms**), and **algorithms** (e.g., **fault detectors**, **supervisory controllers**, and **state estimators**). The orchestration involves:
  1. **Calibration algorithms** updating the **CPS and environmental models** with real-time sensor data to refine parameter estimates.
  2. **Monitors** (likely implemented in the Raspberry Pi) verifying **safety and optimal performance** of the PT.
  3. **Human or algorithmic intervention** for corrective actions when violations (e.g., temperature deviations) are detected.
  The architecture is **closed-loop**, with the DT continuously synchronizing with the PT via sensor feedback. No explicit mention of **resource management** (e.g., cloud-edge hybrid) or **system-wide coordination patterns** (e.g., microservices, service-oriented) is provided, but the **modularity of models** (two distinct abstractions) suggests **plug-and-play extensibility** for future components."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle stages in the provided context include **pre-deployment modeling and simulation phases**, where abstract representations (e.g., CAD models for geometry or differential equation-based dynamic models for physics) are developed to capture specific aspects of the Physical Twin (PT). These models are later integrated into the DT framework during deployment, where they are continuously updated via **calibration algorithms** to align with real-time sensor data from the PT. The lifecycle also encompasses **operational monitoring**, where the DT employs algorithms (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) to assess system performance against predefined safety and optimality criteria. When deviations are detected, the system transitions into **adaptive control phases**, where human or algorithmic interventions modify the PT’s behavior or parameters. The lifecycle governance is implicit in the iterative nature of calibration and monitoring, ensuring the DT remains synchronized with the PT’s evolving state. No explicit governance frameworks (e.g., ISO standards for lifecycle management) are mentioned, but the process relies on **real-time data-driven feedback loops** between the PT and DT, as illustrated in Figure 1."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin emphasizes **model fidelity** and **validity** through:
  - **Fidelity requirements**: Models must accurately represent PT behavior under operating conditions. For example, the incubator’s 4-parameter thermal model was chosen over a 2-parameter model due to its superior prediction of thermal inertia, demonstrating higher fidelity.
  - **Validation and verification methods**: Empirical experiments (e.g., comparing model predictions to real-world PT performance) and iterative refinement (e.g., adjusting free parameters based on sensor data).
  - **Uncertainty sources**: Model inaccuracies arise from discrepancies between simulated and real-world conditions (e.g., environmental factors not fully captured in the model).
  - **Error detection and correction**: Calibration algorithms dynamically adjust model parameters (e.g., thermal inertia) using real-time sensor data to minimize errors.
  - **Quality assurance processes**: Continuous monitoring of PT performance via the DT framework, with violations triggering corrective actions (e.g., human or algorithmic interventions to adjust the physical system). The incubator case study’s open-source hardware/software enables reproducible validation of these processes."
]

    base:desc "The DT’s technical network infrastructure for the incubator case study relies on a **cyber-physical system (CPS) architecture**, where the **Raspberry Pi** serves as the central node connecting the cyber (software/controller) and physical (sensors/actuators) components. The **communication protocols** are not explicitly specified, but typical CPS implementations use **serial (UART), I2C, or TCP/IP** for sensor-actuator communication, with **Controller Area Network (CAN)** or **Ethernet** for broader system integration. The **network topology** is likely a **star topology** (Raspberry Pi as the hub) or a **hybrid topology** if additional nodes (e.g., cloud servers) are involved. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are not quantified, but the system prioritizes **low-latency, high-reliability** communication for real-time control (e.g., temperature regulation). **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not detailed, though the use of **multiple temperature sensors** (Document 2, Section 2.1) suggests sensor redundancy for fault tolerance. **Security protocols** are not mentioned, but standard CPS practices may include **encryption (TLS), authentication (OAuth), and access control** to protect against cyber threats."
]

    base:desc "The incubator DT is deployed on a **hybrid hosting infrastructure**, primarily leveraging **on-premise edge computing** (Raspberry Pi) for real-time processing and control, with potential **cloud integration** for advanced analytics or remote monitoring. instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system integrates **safety-critical monitoring mechanisms** through its **monitoring algorithms**, which continuously assess whether the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its Physical Twin (PT) are operating within safe and optimal parameters. The system employs **calibration algorithms** to update models with real-time data, ensuring accurate representation of the PT's state. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implied in the DT framework, where detected violations (e.g., deviations from safe operating conditions) trigger human or algorithmic interventions to modify the PT's behavior. The DT's **supervisory controllers** and **state estimators** further enhance safety by dynamically adjusting system responses based on real-time data and model predictions. However, the documents do not specify explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, access control, or authentication systems), threat mitigation strategies (e.g., intrusion detection), or compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR, ISO 27001). The **Incubator case study** lacks detailed references to data governance, privacy protection, or consent management, focusing instead on functional modeling and calibration."
]
